description: In a living room, Chandler and Ross sit on couches around a coffee table, while Phoebe sits on an armchair. Rachel, wearing an apron over a dark long-sleeve shirt, walks toward them holding a plate of brownies.
Rachel extends the plate and says, "Here you go, Phoebes. Who else wants one of my special homemade brownies?"
Phoebe takes a brownie as Ross leans forward, pointing at the plate, and says, "I will have one." Chandler raises his hand to take one as well.

Phoebe bites into her brownie, immediately gasps, and holds a hand over her mouth. Chandler retracts his hand and says, "Yeah, I'm not going to have one," as Ross also lowers his hand. Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel lowers the plate slightly, looking toward Phoebe.
Phoebe, still pressing her hand against her jaw, says, "No, it's just my tooth." Rachel gestures toward her with one hand.
Ross holds his brownie in one hand and gestures toward Phoebe, his brow furrowing.
Ross says, "So what's the matter? You need a dentist? I've got a good one." Phoebe shakes her head slightly and replies, "Thanks. I have a good one too."
Phoebe shifts in her chair and raises her hands.
Phoebe gestures with her hands moving outward and says, "I can't see him."
Chandler leans forward, gesturing with his hand. Audience laughter sounds.
Chandler says, "See, that is the problem with invisible dentists."
Chandler raises both hands near his chest amid audience laughter.
Chandler wriggles his fingers while gesturing broadly and says, "It's very unsettling to see all those metal tools just flying around."
Ross looks at Chandler, then turns back to Phoebe. Phoebe touches her chest with her hand.
Ross gestures with his hand holding the brownie and asks, "Why? Why can't you go to them?" Phoebe replies, "Because".
Phoebe raises her shoulders and hands as audience laughter plays.
Phoebe says, "every time I go to the dentist, somebody dies." Chandler and Ross look toward her.
Chandler points his hand holding the brownie toward Phoebe.
Chandler says, "That is so weird, because every time I go to the dentist I look down the hygienist blouse."
Rachel stands behind the couch, crossing her arms, and looks down at Phoebe.
Rachel says, "Phoebe".
Phoebe looks up at Rachel and asks, "what?"
Phoebe gestures with an open hand and says, "Yeah. Kristen was my Aunt Mary. And then there was John, my mailman."
Phoebe brings her hands toward her chest and says, "And then my cowboy friend, Albino bob." Rachel lowers her arms to the back of the couch, and audience laughter is heard.
Rachel gestures toward Phoebe with one open hand.
Rachel asks, "And all of these people actually died?" Phoebe nods rapidly and replies, "Yes. While I was in the chair."
Phoebe lifts her hands again.
Phoebe says, "That's why I take such good care of my teeth. Now. You know it's not about oral hygiene. I flossed to save lives," mimicking a flossing motion near her mouth.
Ross turns his head toward Phoebe and leans his arm on the couch.
Ross says, "Phoebes, come on. You didn't kill anybody."
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "These people just happened to die when you went to the dentist. It's just a coincidence."
Phoebe shakes her head and says, "Well, tell that to them. Oh, you can't. They're"
Phoebe says, "dead." Rachel looks downward as audience laughter plays.
The screen turns black, remaining devoid of imagery.

A view outside a storefront shows a window with two large coffee cup decals and text reading "CENTRAL PERK". The setting changes to the interior of the coffee shop, where Ross hands a mug to a man with a light-colored tie standing behind the counter.
Rachel, carrying a coffee pot, approaches the counter as Ross says, "Thanks, Gunther."
Rachel says, "Hi. Can I get a napkin too?" The man behind the counter responds, "Oh, like you don't already have," holding out a napkin to her before turning away.
Audience laughter sounds as Phoebe sits on an orange couch in the coffee shop and brings a red apple to her mouth, taking a bite.
Phoebe pulls the apple away and touches her jaw, saying, "Ow."
Rachel, sitting in an armchair opposite Phoebe, leans forward.
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "Phoebes, you're in pain. Would you just go to the dentist? Just go."
Phoebe looks at her and replies, "All right, fine. But if you're my next"
Phoebe points her finger toward Rachel.
Phoebe says, "victim don't come back as a poltergeist who, like, sucks me into the TV set."
Rachel adjusts her hair with one hand and leans back slightly amid audience laughter.
Rachel says, "I promise. Although, don't feel like you can't visit."
Joey walks into the coffee shop wearing a dark leather jacket, accompanied by Monica and another woman, moving toward the couch.
Joey stops behind the couch, looks around, and asks, "Hey, is Chandler here?"
Ross, sitting on the couch, shakes his head.
Ross says, "No. No, he's not."
Monica stands near the couch, leaning forward slightly toward Ross and Phoebe.
Monica says, "Joey just saw Janice kissing her ex husband." Phoebe’s jaw drops open as she says, "What?"
Ross gestures with his hand and asks, "Oh. So, what are you gonna do?" while the audience reacts with a gasp and light laughter.
Ross continues, "I mean, how are you gonna tell Chandler?"
Joey gestures with his hands outward and replies, "I was thinking about that, and I think the best way would be to not"
Ross shakes his head and shifts his body toward Joey amid audience laughter.
Ross says, "Joey, you can't keep this to yourself. If you know about this, you have to tell him. It'll kill him."
Joey raises his hands in a questioning gesture.
Joey says, "I mean, it'll just kill him."
Phoebe looks at Joey and says, "Well, you could wait till I go to the dentist. Maybe I'll kill him."
Audience laughter plays. The setting changes to an exterior shot of a large brick apartment building. The scene then transitions inside an apartment where Joey walks toward a window.
Joey looks out the window, gesturing with his hand, and says, "Oh, ugly Naked Guy's using his new hammock. It's like a Play DOH fat factory"
Audience laughter continues. In the background, Chandler stands holding a phone to his ear.
Inside an apartment, Phoebe stands near the front door wearing a green coat. Rachel and Monica sit on couches in the background.
Phoebe turns toward Rachel and Monica, gesturing with both hands. "Going to the dentist. So listen, okay? Just be on the lookout for anything that you could fall into or that" she says.
"Can fall on you or" Phoebe continues, her hands moving downward.
Phoebe raises her hands slightly again.
"Just look out, okay?" Phoebe says, gesturing with open palms.
"And also, I just want to tell you all" Phoebe says, placing a hand on her chest and then stepping backward toward the door.
"That's" Phoebe says, her voice breaking. She turns and hurriedly exits through the door. Rachel and Monica watch her leave. Audience laughter plays.
Ross walks through the front door, wearing a black sweater.
Ross steps into the living room. "Okay, I have a problem. I have to go into work for a few hours," he says. Joey sits at the kitchen table in the background.
Ross gestures toward the women on the couch. "Some kids messed up the Homo sapien display," he says.
Joey turns to look at Ross. "What do they do?" Joey asks. Ross replies, "Well, they painted over the word sapien, for one thing." Audience laughter plays.
Rachel and Monica watch Ross from the couch.
Ross raises his hands, gesturing. "Then they rearranged the figures. Let's just leave it at that," he says, lowering his hands. Audience laughter plays.
Monica leans forward on the couch.
"So do you want me to watch Ben for you?" Monica asks. Ross nods, saying, "Yes, that's what I was going to ask. Thank you." Rachel sits up straight, touching her chest. "Whoa, wait," Rachel says.
"Hello? What about me?" Rachel asks, gesturing to herself. Ross looks at her. "You want to watch Ben?" he asks, his eyebrows raised.
Rachel points to herself and nods. Monica gestures toward Rachel, her eyebrows raised. Audience laughter plays.
Ross smiles slightly. "Yes, that'd be great. No, I just asked Monica because I know how empty her life is," Ross says, chuckling briefly.
Monica's jaw drops open. Audience laughter plays.
Joey raises a hand. "Hey, Ross. I got a science question," Joey says. Ross turns to look at him.
Joey gestures with his raised hand. "If the Homo sapiens were in fact, Homo sapiens?" he says.
Joey lowers his hand and looks at Ross.
"Is that why they're extinct?" Joey asks, spreading his arms slightly.
Rachel covers her mouth with her hand. Monica looks downward. Ross stares toward Joey, his facial expression remaining static. Audience laughter plays.

Ross looks down briefly, then back up. "Joey, Homo sapiens are people," he says, his volume lower than his previous line. Joey leans back, shrugging. "Hey, I'm not judging," Joey replies. Audience laughter plays.
Ross turns and walks toward the hallway. The setting changes to the kitchen area. Rachel holds a young boy, Ben, in her arms.
Monica stands at the counter in the background. Rachel holds up a spoon toward Ben. "Look, Penny spoon," Rachel says.
Ben's gaze remains forward.
"Spoon," Rachel repeats.
Rachel lowers the spoon and looks at Ben.
Rachel exhales audibly. "Come on. All right, you know what? I think he's bored," she says, turning toward Monica.
Monica sets down a cup and approaches Rachel and Ben.
"Going to play the airplane game?" Monica asks, taking Ben from Rachel. "You want to show Rachel, come here. You're going to do something fun?" she says, holding Ben in front of her.
"Okay. Wee" Monica says, lifting Ben slightly.
Rachel stands nearby, smiling and clapping her hands together.
"hoo," Monica says as she swings Ben upward. Ben's head hits a wooden support beam overhead. A loud thud is audible.
Rachel gasps and covers her mouth with both hands. Audience laughter plays.
Monica immediately pulls Ben close to her chest, her eyes widening. Rachel keeps her hands over her mouth.
"Brave. You're so brave. Yes, you are. You're so brave," Monica says rapidly, patting Ben's back.
"Okay, he's fine. He's fine. Let's just put him down," Rachel says, reaching out to take Ben from Monica.
Rachel holds Ben. "Come here, babe. Yes. You're such a good boy," she says. Audience laughter plays.
Rachel holds Ben, looking at Monica.
"How could you do that to him?" Rachel asks, her volume higher than her previous line.
Monica gestures rapidly with her hands.
"What is he going to say?" Rachel asks.
Monica steps closer. "He's not going to say anything because we're not going to tell him," Monica says. Rachel asks, "We're not?"
"No, we're not," Monica states firmly. "All right, I like that," Rachel responds. Audience laughter plays.
Monica gestures toward Rachel.
"So we're okay," Rachel says.
Monica nods rapidly. "We're okay. We're okay, aren't we?" she says.
Rachel touches Ben's head. "No, we're not okay. We're not okay. There's a bump. There's a bump," Rachel says, her volume rising on the final phrases.
Monica touches Ben's head. "Oh, my God," Monica says.

"Push it in. Push it in," Monica says, pressing her finger against Ben's head. "I cannot push it in," Rachel says, brushing Monica's hand away and sitting down on the couch with Ben. Audience laughter plays.
Monica stands over them, wringing her hands.
"Okay, we're gonna need a distraction," Monica says, stepping closer to Rachel. "Okay, that's it," she adds.

Monica points a finger upward. "Okay. I got it. Okay, the second that Ross walks in that door yeah?" she says. Rachel nods. "I want you to take him back to your bedroom and you do whatever it" Monica continues, gesturing widely.
"is that you do that makes him go ree," Monica says, her pitch rising sharply as she imitates a noise.
Rachel looks at Monica, her mouth slightly open. Ben sits quietly next to Rachel. Audience laughter plays.
"Or" Rachel says, raising a finger.
Monica looks at Rachel expectantly.
"we could put a hat on his head," Rachel suggests. "A hat. Yes, we need a hat. We need a hat," Monica agrees rapidly, tapping her hands together.
"Where are we going to find a tiny little hat?" Rachel asks. Monica points toward the hallway, saying "Whoa, whoa, whoa."
"I'll get rainy day bear," Monica says, walking toward the hallway.
Monica walks away. Rachel watches her go.
"I guess he'll know what to do," Monica says as she walks out of view. Monica returns holding a teddy bear wearing a yellow raincoat and hat. "Oh, my God, you're a genius," Rachel says.
Rachel grabs the bear and tries to pull the yellow hat off. "Oh, God. It's kind of sewn on, though," Rachel says. "Give it. Give it," Monica says, pulling at the hat with Rachel.
"Okay," Monica and Rachel say simultaneously as they struggle with the bear.
Rachel and Monica continue to pull forcefully on the bear's yellow hat.
Ben sits on a couch holding a small toy. Monica and Rachel stand nearby, with Monica holding a teddy bear.
Rachel presses her hands against the sides of her head. "It's just like a bloodbath in here today," she says.
The setting changes to an aerial view of a busy city street with traffic.
Outside on the street, Chandler stops in front of a jewelry store window display. Joey continues walking a few steps before stopping.
Chandler points at the window display and says, "Hold on a second. Hold on a second."
Joey turns and walks back toward Chandler.
"Do you think these pearls are nice?" Chandler asks, gesturing to the window.
Chandler looks at Joey.
Joey looks at the display and replies, "I'd really prefer a mountain bike." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler turns his gaze from Joey back to the display.
"Janice's birthday is coming up. I want to get her something special," Chandler says.
Chandler gestures toward the store entrance.
As Chandler steps toward the door, Joey grabs his arm. "Come in here with whoa," they say with overlapping voices, accompanied by audience laughter.
Joey raises his hands, holding Chandler back.
"You want to get her something special?" Joey asks.
Joey gestures with his hands. "Get her flowers. Get her candy," he says.
"Get her gum. Girls love gum," Joey adds, tapping his chest. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks at Joey.
Chandler mimics chewing vigorously. "That's a good idea. Dear Janice, have a hub above a birthday," he says over audience laughter.
Chandler drops the mimicry and looks directly at Joey.
"I would like to get her something serious," Chandler says.
"Oh, you want something serious? You know what you should do? You should get her one of those," Joey says, pointing a finger and pausing.
"barium enemas," Joey finishes. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler stares at Joey in silence.
"Those are dead serious," Joey says, followed by audience laughter.
Joey gestures with his hands again.
"And you know she'll never get one for herself," Joey says, nodding.
Chandler closes his eyes briefly and exhales slightly.
Chandler points at Joey. "Look, I'm going to go in here and you don't buy me anything, ever," he says, stepping toward the door.
Joey grabs Chandler's arms and pulls him back. "No, you can't. You can't. You can't buy her pearls. You," Joey says rapidly, while audience laughter is audible.
"just can't," Joey concludes, maintaining his grip on Chandler's arms.
"Why not?" Chandler asks.
Chandler looks at Joey, waiting.
Joey rubs his hand over his hair. "Oh, God. Okay, here's the thing," he says.
Joey hesitates, gesturing with his hand.
"This is the thing," Joey says.
"Okay, the thing is what is the thing? Okay," Chandler replies.
Joey looks down briefly before meeting Chandler's gaze.
"I went down to the Mattress King's showroom and I saw Janice kissing her ex husband," Joey says.
Chandler's mouth falls open slightly.
"What?" Chandler asks.
Joey looks at Chandler steadily.
"They were in his office," Joey says softly. Audience laughter plays briefly.
Chandler looks away, his face tense, then looks back at Joey.
"She wouldn't do that. She's with me," Chandler says. "I'm telling you, man, I saw it," Joey insists.
"Yeah, well, you're wrong, okay? You're wrong," Chandler argues. "I'm not wrong," Joey replies.
"I wish I was," Joey adds quietly.
Chandler stares at Joey in silence.
"I'm sorry," Joey says softly.
Chandler looks down, remaining still for a few seconds, then looks back up at Joey.
Joey tilts his head. "Bet that barium enema doesn't sound so bad," he says over audience laughter.
Chandler's gaze remains fixed on Joey, his lips pressed together.

Now in the apartment kitchen, Ben sits in a high chair wearing a yellow raincoat and a yellow hat with a clear plastic visor. Monica stands next to him, and Rachel leans on the table. "See, it just makes more sense as an ensemble," Monica says.
Monica touches Ben's hat.
"Besides, it takes the focus off the hat," Monica adds.
Phoebe hurries through the door, wearing a green coat and carrying a bag.
"Oh, you're alive. You're alive," Phoebe says, smiling and gesturing widely.
Rachel smiles at Phoebe.
"See? Phoebes. I promised you no one would die, didn't I?" Rachel says. Audience laughter is audible.
"Yes. Well, we'll see about that," Phoebe says, taking a white cordless phone from the counter. "Can I use your phone? If you want to call everyone I know?" "Sure," Rachel replies.
"We have no money. Go ahead," Rachel says.
Phoebe dials and holds the phone to her ear.
"Hey, you're not dead. Okay. See you," Phoebe says, then hangs up.
"You," Ben babbles.
Monica feeds Ben a piece of cereal from a container on the table.
"Monica," Ben says, followed by audience laughter.
Monica's eyes widen. "Oh, my God," she says.
Monica turns to look at Rachel.
"He just said my name," Monica says.
"Did you hear that?" Monica asks. "Yeah," Rachel replies.
Rachel leans in close to Ben.
"Monica bang," Ben says. Audience laughter plays.
Monica covers her face with her hand, leaning heavily against a wooden pillar.
"Okay, I heard that," Rachel says.
Monica looks at Rachel with wide eyes.
"Did he just say, Monica bang?" Rachel asks. "Oh, my God," Monica says.
"He's going to rat me out," Rachel says, her voice rising in pitch. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel leans toward Ben again.
"Monica bang," Ben repeats.
Rachel gasps and stands up straight.
"Sweetie, sweetie, you need to stop saying that now. That's no big deal," Rachel says, waving her hands.
"It's not even worth mentioning," Rachel continues rapidly.
Rachel points at herself.
"You see, we all do it all the time. See?" Rachel says.
Rachel steps to the wooden pillar. "Watch this. Ben, Ben, Ben," Rachel says, then intentionally bumps her head against the pillar. "Ow," she says.
Monica moves to the pillar and bumps her head against it. "Monica bang," she says.
Rachel bumps her head again. "Everybody bang," she says over audience laughter.
Monica bumps her head. "Ben bang," she says.
Rachel bumps her head against the pillar again.
"Rachel bang," Rachel says.
"Bang, Rachel bang," Monica says, bumping her head. Audience laughter continues.
Rachel bumps her head again.
"Oh, isn't it?" Rachel asks, bending forward with Monica as they both laugh, accompanied by audience laughter.
Ben watches them, motionless in his high chair.
"We all do it," Rachel says, standing up straight and rubbing her head.
"Okay, I'm stopping now. You okay?" Rachel asks, looking at Monica.
Monica steps away from the pillar, rubbing her forehead.
"Oh, yeah. You know, if it's not a headboard, it's just not worth it," Rachel says, walking away. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to a living room. Chandler sits motionless in a black leather recliner, holding an open book, his gaze fixed forward.

In an apartment kitchen and living area, Chandler sits in a black recliner, holding an open book and looking forward. Janice opens the front door and enters, wearing a leopard-print jumpsuit under a long red coat and carrying a bag. Audience laughter sounds.
Janice closes the door, smiling. "How's my bing a ling?" she asks.
Chandler tosses his book into his lap, stands up quickly, and places his hands on his hips. Audience laughter plays.
"I don't know," Chandler says.
Chandler tilts his head slightly.
"You tell me," Chandler continues.
Chandler steps forward, walking past the recliner toward the kitchen counter.
Janice stands still. Chandler asks, "Anything you want to tell me?"
Chandler stops near the counter.
Chandler gestures with his right hand, saying, "Because".
Chandler lowers his right hand back to his hip.
Chandler points his finger at Janice, stating, "you tell me."
Janice's mouth opens slightly, her eyebrows raising.
Janice asks, "Why are your eyes so wide?"
Chandler steps closer to her, his eyes widened, and replies, "You tell me."
Chandler keeps his eyes wide, looking at her.
Chandler says rapidly, "Maybe it's because I was just fooling around with my".
Chandler shakes his head, pointing at himself then at her, and shouts, "No, no, that was you." Audience laughter sounds.
Janice gasps, bringing her hands up near her chest.
Janice asks, "All right. How did you know?" Chandler immediately responds, stepping forward, "Joey told me."
Chandler points his finger at her, saying, "He saw you two kissing in the park,"
Janice squeezes her eyes shut briefly, her shoulders rising. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler lowers his hands and asks, "in his office. How many kisses were there?" Janice replies, her voice tight, "Yes, there was."
Chandler leans forward slightly, keeping his gaze fixed on her.
Chandler shouts, "Why was there kissing? There should be no kissing."
Janice grabs the lapels of her top, her pitch rising as she says, "Oh, I'm sorry, honey."
Janice breathes heavily, pulling on her lapels.
Janice says, her voice breaking, "I'm so, so sorry. I just"
Janice takes rapid, shallow breaths, keeping her hands near her chest.
Janice stammers, "What happened was I can't breathe," her breathing remaining rapid.
Janice paces in a tight circle and asks rapidly, "Can you get me a bag or something?" Chandler turns toward the kitchen area.
Chandler hurries to a small shelf and grabs a brown paper bag.
Chandler holds the bag out to her, saying, "Here."
Janice takes the bag from him.
Chandler says, "There." Janice brings the bag to her face.
Janice holds the bag over her mouth and nose, breathing heavily into it. The bag inflates and deflates with audible crinkling sounds.
Chandler reaches forward for the bag and says, "I'll take it."
Chandler lowers his hands. Janice continues breathing into the bag.
Chandler gestures with both hands, saying, "Look, I've got to know."
Janice keeps breathing into the bag. The crinkling noise continues.
Chandler asks, his volume softer, "Are you finished with me?"
Janice takes a breath from the bag.
Chandler asks, "Are you finished with him?"
Janice continues breathing into the bag, the paper crinkling.
Chandler asks, "Do you still love him?"
Janice breathes into the bag, her eyes on Chandler.
Chandler asks, "Do you still love me?"
Janice lowers the bag slightly from her face.
Chandler says, "All right, look," reaching out and pulling the bag away from her grasp.
Chandler holds the crushed bag in his right hand.
Chandler says, "I'm going to need an actual answer here, okay?"
Chandler gestures briefly with his left hand.
Chandler asks, "So which is it?"
Janice brings her bare hands up to cover her mouth.
Chandler asks, "Him or me?"
Janice breathes heavily through her hands, her shoulders rising and falling. Audience laughter plays.
Janice continues holding her hands over her mouth as Chandler watches her. The scene then becomes completely dark.
The screen remains black with no audio present.
